operating_state: Total revenue reached $254.5 billion for fiscal 2024, representing 5.0% growth driven by membership fee increases to $4.8 billion and comparable sales expansion of 5% across all segments. The company expanded to 890 warehouses globally with 30 new openings while membership grew to 76.2 million paid members with Executive penetration reaching 73.3% of worldwide net sales. International operations generated 28% of net sales with broad strength across regions, while e-commerce accelerated 16% representing approximately 7% of total sales.
cost_structure: Gross margin improved 35 basis points to 10.92% driven by membership fee leverage, favorable product mix shifts, and warehouse ancillary business performance including e-commerce and gasoline operations. SG&A expenses increased 6 basis points to 9.14% of sales primarily from wage increases implemented in July 2024 raising starting wages to $19.50 per hour, partially offset by operational efficiencies and scale benefits.
material_events: Membership fees increased effective September 1, 2024, raising Gold Star and Business memberships to $65 annually and Executive membership to $130, expected to generate approximately $370 million in additional revenue over two years. The company paid $8.6 billion in total dividends including a $15 per share special dividend and increased quarterly dividend 14% to $1.16 per share. Share repurchases totaled $698 million under the $4.0 billion authorization program.
macro_exposures: Government tariff actions particularly between China and the United States continue affecting merchandise costs with exposure dependent on goods type and timing of implementations. Foreign currency fluctuations negatively impacted net sales by $474 million with ongoing exposure through international operations comprising 28% of total sales, managed through forward foreign-exchange contracts. Gasoline price deflation reduced net sales by $917 million despite volume increases, while commodity price exposure includes electricity and natural gas costs partially mitigated through fixed-price contracts.
forward_outlook: Management plans to open up to 29 new warehouses in fiscal 2025 with capital expenditures expected at approximately $4.7 billion focused on warehouse construction, remodels, and technology infrastructure investments. The September 2024 membership fee increases are projected to benefit fiscal 2025 by $190 million primarily in the latter half of the year. Strategic emphasis remains on international expansion, technology investments for digital capabilities enhancement, and expanding Kirkland Signature private-label penetration.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple legal proceedings include California labor law class actions, opioid litigation, privacy law violations, and False Claims Act investigation regarding prescription medications with unpredictable outcomes requiring potential substantial payments. Cybersecurity threats pose increasing risks to systems and member data amid expanding digital operations, while supply chain disruptions from geopolitical events could impact merchandise availability and costs. Self-insurance liabilities of $1.6 billion rely on actuarial assumptions that could vary significantly from historical experience.
